ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general coffee beverage class; no specific iced white chocolate mocha subclass exists. Closest defensible parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers coffee-based beverages broadly; no specific iced white chocolate mocha code exists. Closest defensible category used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for iced white chocolate mocha; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

allergens: soy (white chocolate sauce may contain soy lecithin)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard medium-sized serving of iced white chocolate mocha is approximately 355 g (12 fl oz), as commonly served at coffee chains in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Iced White Chocolate Mocha — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Iced White Chocolate Mocha (cr_id G00010292). Values represent the as-served beverage per 100 g. Saturated fat not reported by HPB for this entry; estimated from white chocolate sauce and whole milk composition using USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy) as a secondary reference.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous estimate based on sweetened milk-based coffee beverage with added sugar syrup
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for iced white chocolate mocha. GI estimated at 62 (medium) based on the closest analogous entry: sweetened milk-based coffee beverage with sugar syrup (GI ~60–65) from the University of Sydney GI Database. The dominant glycaemic driver is the white chocolate sauce (sucrose and glucose syrup). Carbohydrate per serving = 36.8 g (10.36 g/100 g × 355 g). GL = (62 × 36.8) / 100 = 22.8.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (9.94 g/100 g) in a sugar-sweetened beverage independently elevates blood pressure risk; potassium is negligible at 5 mg/100 g, providing no offsetting benefit
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this beverage because its sugar load (9.94 g/100 g, exceeding the 5 g/100 g beverage sugar threshold) independently raises blood pressure risk per AHA 2018 guidance, and the near-zero potassium content provides no compensatory vasodilatory benefit; requesting sugar-free white chocolate syrup is the primary mitigation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 9.94 g sugar/100 g, zero dietary fibre, and a medium-high glycaemic load of 22.8 per serving, causing rapid postprandial glucose elevation
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this beverage in its standard form because the 9.94 g/100 g sugar content with zero fibre constitutes a sugar-sweetened beverage that triggers rapid glycaemic excursion; substituting sugar-free white chocolate syrup and requesting a smaller serving size are the primary risk-reduction strategies.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High added sugar from white chocolate sauce contributes to dyslipidaemia and visceral adiposity risk; saturated fat from cocoa butter in white chocolate sauce adds modest cardiovascular burden
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk should limit this beverage due to its high added sugar content (9.94 g/100 g) which promotes triglyceride elevation and visceral fat accumulation, and the saturated fat contribution from white chocolate sauce cocoa butter; choosing an unsweetened espresso with plain low-fat milk is a substantially lower-risk alternative.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pro-inflammatory profile: high sugar, saturated fat from cocoa butter, zero fibre, and no anti-inflammatory micronutrients
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this beverage because the combination of high sugar (9.94 g/100 g), saturated fat from white chocolate cocoa butter, and complete absence of anti-inflammatory factors (no omega-3, no polyphenols, no fibre >3 g, no anti-inflammatory spices) creates a net pro-inflammatory dietary load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Iced White Chocolate Mocha is a cold espresso-based coffee beverage made by combining freshly pulled espresso shots with white chocolate sauce, cold milk, and ice. It is served without whipped cream in its standard Singapore café format. The drink has a sweet, creamy, and mildly coffee-forward flavour profile, with the white chocolate sauce providing a rich cocoa-butter sweetness that dominates the espresso bitterness. The texture is smooth and liquid with a slightly viscous quality from the dissolved sugar syrup. Per 100 g, the beverage provides 42 kcal, 10.36 g carbohydrate (of which 9.94 g is sugar), 0.25 g fat, and negligible dietary fibre. The high sugar content (9.94 g/100 g) classifies this as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB), making it clinically significant for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease. The glycaemic load per standard 355 g serving is approximately 22.8, placing it in the high-concern category for glycaemic management. White chocolate sauce is the primary source of added sugar and saturated fat (cocoa butter); the espresso base contributes caffeine and trace polyphenols. This beverage is widely available at Western-style coffee chains in Singapore and is popular as an all-day café drink. It is vegetarian and halal-suitable but not vegan due to milk content.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010292, confidence 0.95), which is the primary approved source for Singapore food records. The match is exact for this commercially served beverage. Protein value corrected from HPB-reported 0.0 g to 0.9 g based on milk content cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central (whole milk, SR Legacy) as a secondary reference. Saturated fat estimated from component analysis. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database.
notes: 1. PROTEIN CORRECTION: The input record reported protein_g as 0.0. This is implausible for a beverage containing milk and has been corrected to 0.9 g/100 g based on USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy whole milk data (approximately 3.2 g protein/100 g milk, diluted by espresso and ice in the final beverage). This correction is documented as a source adaptation. 2. ZERO-MACRO JUSTIFICATION: dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB source — this is a liquid beverage with no whole-food fibre components. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero per HPB source — the beverage contains negligible cholesterol at this dilution. magnesium_mg is confirmed zero per HPB source. 3. NEGATIVE SENTINEL VALUES: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific iced white chocolate mocha beverage are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for G00010292, and no equivalent breakdown is available in USDA FoodData Central for this exact commercial formulation as of 2026. 4. SATURATED FAT: Estimated at 0.15 g/100 g based on white chocolate sauce cocoa butter content and whole milk fat contribution, cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. 5. GI NOTE: No direct GI measurement exists for iced white chocolate mocha; GI of 62 is an analogous estimate based on sweetened milk-based coffee beverages from the University of Sydney GI Database. 6. ONTOLOGY: FoodOn and FoodEx2 codes are approximate matches to the closest defensible parent categories; no exact codes exist for this specific beverage formulation.
